title: Word Break
description: Given a string s and a dictionary of strings wordDict, return true if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words. Note that the same word in the dictionary may be reused multiple times in the segmentation.
constraints: s and wordDict[i] consist of only lowercase English letters
All the strings of wordDict are unique
optimal_time: O(n^2 * m) where m=avg word length
optimal_space: O(n + W) where W=word dict size
thinking_questions: If the substring s[0..j-1] can be segmented, and s[j..i-1] is in the dictionary, what does that tell you about s[0..i-1]?
How do you efficiently check if a substring is in the dictionary?

hints.L1: dp[i] = True if s[0..i-1] can be segmented. For each position i, look back at all j where dp[j]=True and s[j..i] is in wordDict.
hints.L2: Convert wordDict to a set for O(1) lookups. dp[0]=True (empty). For i in 1..n: for j in 0..i: if dp[j] and s[j:i] in word_set: dp[i]=True.
hints.L3:
# Word Break — O(n^2) time (ignoring string hashing), O(n) space
# KEY INSIGHT: dp[i] = can we break s[0:i]?
# dp[i] = True if any dp[j]=True and s[j:i] in wordDict

def wordBreak(s, wordDict):
    word_set = set(wordDict)
    n = len(s)
    dp = [False] * (n + 1)
    dp[0] = True  # empty string always segmentable

    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        for j in range(i):
            if dp[j] and s[j:i] in word_set:
                dp[i] = True
                break  # found one valid split, no need to continue

    return dp[n]
# OPTIMIZATION: limit j range based on max word length in dict

follow_up_questions: How would you return all valid segmentations? (Word Break II)
How can you optimize using max word length?
Could you solve this with BFS or DFS + memoization?
solution_notes: dp[i] = can break s[0:i]. Set for O(1) lookup. For each i, check all j < i where dp[j]=True and s[j:i] in set.
